title: 10 best herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected area. The tincture's ability to relax muscles and improve circulation also plays a crucial role in alleviating stiffness and improving range of motion. Additionally, highbush cranberry contains antioxidants that help to protect against cellular damage and promote healing, ultimately contributing to the tincture's effectiveness in addressing the chronic pain and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Highbush cranberry tincture helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected joint, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Analgesic and anodyne properties: The tincture's analgesic properties help relieve pain, while its anodyne properties reduce discomfort and numbness, making it a natural remedy for managing frozen shoulder pain.

Immune system modulation: Highbush cranberry's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate the immune response, which may contribute to reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected joint, promoting healing and pain relief.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and anti-catabolic effects, which may help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint, while also promoting collagen synthesis and joint health.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in frozen shoulder.
Barks: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 pounds of vaccinium macrocarpon berries in late summer when they are ripe and fully colored.
Clean the berries thoroughly under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 20-30 pounds of cleaned berries with 80-100 proof vodka or 95% ethanol in a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected joint. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby minimizing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant effects help protect damaged tissues from further harm, allowing for faster recovery and reduced stiffness. By targeting these underlying causes of frozen shoulder, turmeric tinctures can provide natural relief and improve range of motion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for improved range of motion.

Pain-relieving property: Turmeric's analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties can help alleviate chronic pain and discomfort in the affected joint, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of frozen shoulder, while also promoting overall joint health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the condition's development and progression.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid that inhibits pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduces pain associated with frozen shoulder by modulating the body's inflammatory response.
Tumerone (Curcumenol): A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain and reduce stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Although less commonly used than rhizomes, roots of Curcuma longa are also used in some tinctures as they still contain curcumin and other beneficial compounds that aid in pain relief.

Leaves: Some tinctures might incorporate leaves of Curcuma longa, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that complement the effects of curcumin, although their use is less documented than that of rhizomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa roots in late summer early fall when they are fully matured and dry.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a warm air circulation oven at 100°f for 3 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Steep 100g of chopped curcuma longa roots in 500ml of high-proof vodka at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid from the root pieces using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work together to decrease inflammation and relax tense muscles, allowing for a greater range of motion. Additionally, ginger's warming properties can help to increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected joint, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of ginger tincture help to numb the pain and discomfort caused by frozen shoulder, providing relief from aching and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant: Ginger tincture's muscle-relaxing properties can help to reduce tension in the surrounding muscles and connective tissue, which can contribute to improved range of motion and reduced stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, are bioactive compounds found in ginger that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Shogaols: Shogaols are also bioactive compounds in ginger that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation, as well as improve joint mobility in frozen shoulder.

Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, are polyphenolic compounds in ginger that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation, pain, and oxidative stress associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are also a popular choice for making tinctures due to their rich content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make tinctures due to their ability to enhance blood circulation and reduce swelling in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh zingiber officinale roots weighing approximately 250 grams for preparation.
Chop the roots into small pieces and then dry them in a single layer for two days.
Combine the dried zingiber officinale roots with 1000 milliliters of 35% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for a minimum of 14 days shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The herbal extract has natural analgesic properties that can block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from discomfort and soreness. Additionally, devil's claw may also improve range of motion and flexibility by reducing inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, allowing for greater mobility and reduced stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Devil's claw tincture contains iridoid glycosides that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint.

Pain-relieving properties: The tincture's analgesic compounds, such as harpagoside, may help to block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic shoulder pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Muscle relaxant properties: Devil's claw's muscle-relaxing properties can help to reduce muscle spasms and tension in the shoulder region, which is a common symptom of frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Harpagide: A phenylethanoid glycoside that helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Procumbins: A group of iridoid glycosides that aid in reducing inflammation and pain in frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Harpagoside: A phenylethanoid glycoside that contributes to the anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects of Harpagophytum procumbens, helping to alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of harpagoside, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
Stems: Stems of Harpagophytum procumbens contain compounds that aid in pain relief and reduction of inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Harpagophytum procumbens leaves are used in some tinctures as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of harpagophytum procumbens in early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Chop 250g of the cleaned roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Steep the chopped roots in 750ml of 30% ethanol for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. When taken internally, salicin reduces inflammation and pain in the joints, muscles, and connective tissue, providing relief from the stiffness and limited range of motion associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, white willow's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce scarring and adhesions that can contribute to chronic pain and discomfort in the affected joint.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tincture contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds help to reduce swelling, ease stiffness, and protect the joint from further damage caused by frozen shoulder.

Analgesic and antispasmodic effects: White willow tincture's analgesic and antispasmodic properties help to relieve pain, reduce muscle spasms, and improve range of motion in individuals with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A phenolic compound, salicin helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Salicortol: A triterpenoid saponin, salicortol contributes to the anti-inflammatory effects of Salix alba, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Quercetin: A flavonoid, quercetin is known for its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in frozen shoulder, promoting pain relief and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to relax the muscles and reduce stiffness in the affected shoulder.
Stems: The stems of Salix alba contain salicylic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried salix alba bark from a trusted supplier or harvest it sustainably yourself.
Crush the dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine 250 grams of the powdered salix alba bark with 750 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce joint pain and stiffness. The capsaicin in bell peppers works to block the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants in bell pepper tinctures help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the formation of adhesions that can exacerbate frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like turmeric (Curcuma longa) and ginger (Zingiber officinale), which have anti-inflammatory compounds like curcumin and gingerol, may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Herbs like willow bark (Salix alba) and white willow (Salix alba var. caerulea) contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.

Muscle relaxant properties: Herbs like valerian root (Valeriana officinalis) and passionflower (Passiflora incarnata) have been traditionally used to calm muscle spasms and promote relaxation, which may be beneficial in reducing tension and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a vanilloid compound, helps with frozen shoulder by blocking the action of substance P, a neurotransmitter that transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing pain and inflammation.

Capsaicinoid Glycosides (specifically, dihydrocapsaicin and nordihydrocapsaicin): These capsaicinoid glycosides help with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and 5-LOX, which are involved in the inflammatory process.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the severity of frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for frozen shoulder because they contain capsaicin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
Fruits: The fruits are used to make tinctures for frozen shoulder because they are a rich source of capsaicin and other bioactive compounds that aid in pain relief and inflammation reduction.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for frozen shoulder because they contain capsaicin and other beneficial compounds that help alleviate pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh capsicum annuum fruits when they are fully ripe and dry them in a low-temperature oven.
Combine 1 part dried capsicum annuum fruits with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use 5-10 drops per serving as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it targets inflammation and reduces pain. The Boswellia serrata extract in frankincense has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate stiffness and swelling, allowing for increased mobility and range of motion. Additionally, frankincense's analgesic and antispasmodic properties work to reduce muscle spasms and ease discomfort. As a natural and non-invasive solution, frankincense tinctures provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms without the need for surgery or prescription medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, allowing for increased mobility and reduced stiffness.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant activity of frankincense helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to frozen shoulder symptoms such as chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration.
Analgesic and sedative: Frankincense's analgesic and sedative properties can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_constituents: Akhilin Saponins: These saponins help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Terpenoids (Boswellic acids): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

3-Acetyl-11-Keto-β-Boswellic acid (AKBA): This specific boswellic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the shoulder joint by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used for medicinal purposes due to their high resin content, which is responsible for anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried boswellia serrata resin in the summer months when it is most potent.
Cut the resin into small pieces and grind them in a spice grinder to a fine powder using 10-15 minutes of preparation time.
Combine 1 part ground boswellia serrata with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to make the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar every 2-3 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected joint. The natural ingredients in arnica, such as thymol and flavonoids, work to relax muscles, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating stiffness and discomfort. By taking an herbal mountain arnica tincture regularly, individuals can experience relief from frozen shoulder symptoms, allowing for improved mobility and reduced pain, enabling a faster return to normal daily activities.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain, swelling, and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder by decreasing prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which contribute to inflammation.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of arnica tincture help alleviate pain and discomfort in the affected joint, making it an effective natural remedy for managing frozen shoulder pain.
Immune-stimulating: Arnica's immune-stimulating effects may help reduce the severity of frozen shoulder by increasing the body's natural defenses against inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Helenin: Helenin, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress associated with frozen shoulder.
Bornadine: Bornadine, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are commonly used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating frozen shoulder.
Roots: The roots of Arnica montana are also used, as they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Stems: The stems of the plant are occasionally used, as they contain a mix of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds that may contribute to the anti-inflammatory effects of Arnica montana tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the sun.
Clean and dry the arnica montana flowers in a well-ventilated area to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine 1 part of fresh arnica montana flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint. Its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines also minimizes tissue damage, allowing for faster healing and increased mobility. Additionally, St John's Wort's antioxidant effects help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and perpetuate frozen shoulder symptoms. As a natural remedy, it provides relief from stiffness, numbness, and limited range of motion associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort helps the body adapt to stress and inflammation, which can contribute to frozen shoulder symptoms, by reducing oxidative stress and promoting antioxidant defenses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and hyperforin present in St. John's Wort tinctures have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and improving circulation to the affected area.

Pain-relieving properties: St. John's Wort contains hypericin, which has been found to exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound that may help with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain through its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

N-octanoyl-4-hydroxybenzoic acid (N-OHBA): N-OHBA is a phenolic compound that may help with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing pain through its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, may help with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing hypericin and hyperforin, the active compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Containing a higher concentration of hypericin and hyperforin compared to other parts, making them effective for pain relief and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves on a sunny day in summer.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of 80-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the plant material in the ethanol mixture for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it reduces muscle spasms, relaxes joints, and eases stiffness. The herbal extract's sedative properties calm inflammation, allowing for increased blood flow to the affected area. This reduced inflammation enables greater mobility and flexibility in the joint, helping to break down adhesions that can contribute to frozen shoulder. Additionally, valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce pain and discomfort, promoting a more comfortable range of motion and improved overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Valerian's sedative and relaxing properties can help reduce muscle tension and spasm associated with frozen shoulder, promoting pain relief and improving range of motion.

Anti-inflammatory: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the affected joint and surrounding tissue, which is thought to contribute to the development and perpetuation of frozen shoulder.

Pain relief: Valerian's analgesic properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, allowing individuals to move more comfortably and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Isopimpinellin: This sesquiterpene helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain through its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Valerenic acid: This valerenic acid acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms associated with frozen shoulder.
Valeranone: This valerenone has been shown to have a sedative effect and can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are often contributing factors to frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of valerenic acid and valeranone, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: They are often used as a secondary part to the roots, providing a more cost-effective option while still retaining some of the medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried valeriana officinalis roots in the morning when the dew is still present.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife and a cutting board.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the tincture to mature slowly.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a unique blend of adaptogenic compounds that reduce inflammation and stress in the body. By calming the nervous system, ashwagandha relaxes muscle tension and increases range of motion, alleviating stiffness and pain associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative damage caused by inflammation, promoting healthy joint function and reducing scar tissue buildup. As a result, ashwagandha tinctures can provide significant relief from the discomfort and limited mobility of frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic property helps reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of frozen shoulder, allowing the body to relax and recover from inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as withanolides, help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting prostaglandins and leukotrienes.

Immune-modulating property: Ashwagandha's immune-modulating effects can help modulate the inflammatory response and reduce oxidative stress, which may contribute to the development of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ashwagandha are rich in withanolides, a group of compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Ashwagandha's rhizomes contain a higher concentration of withanolides than other parts of the plant, which helps to reduce stiffness and improve mobility in patients with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried withania somnifera roots and stems from reputable sources.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife ensuring uniform size.
Combine the chopped plant material with 190 proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling to the top.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place shaking the jar twice daily to ensure efficient extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint. The tincture's ability to relax muscle spasms also alleviates stiffness and rigidity, allowing for increased mobility and range of motion. Additionally, its analgesic effects provide instant relief from discomfort and numbness, making it an effective natural remedy for managing frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, making it easier to move and relieving discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: As an antioxidant, Ginkgo biloba helps protect the joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to Frozen Shoulder development and exacerbation.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels improves circulation in the affected area, helping to increase oxygen and nutrient delivery to the tissues and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that have analgesic and anti-arthritic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder such as pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or air drying method to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Prepare the tincture by combining 1 part dried ginkgo biloba with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar and steeping for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and transfer it to a dark glass bottle, labeling it with ingredients and date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it targets the inflammation and discomfort associated with this condition. The beta-carotene and other antioxidants in saw palmetto work to reduce inflammation, which is a common characteristic of frozen shoulder. Additionally, saw palmetto's ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) may help alleviate symptoms such as pain and stiffness by reducing the formation of scar tissue that can contribute to restricted mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Saw palmetto contains anti-inflammatory fatty acids that may help reduce inflammation in the affected shoulder joint, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Prostaglandin inhibiting properties: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that contribute to inflammation, may help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint.

Androgen-modulating effects: Saw palmetto's ability to modulate androgen hormones, such as testosterone, may help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder by reducing muscle tension and improving range of motion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saw palmetto roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain, making them a suitable choice for frozen shoulder treatment.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant contain fatty acids and phytosterols that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh serenoa repens leaves and stems from the wild or cultivate them in your garden for two weeks to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to release its essential oils and resins for maximum extraction efficiency.
Combine one part chopped plant material with three parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create a maceration mixture, allowing it to steep for four hours.
Strain the maceration mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids and discarding the remaining plant material.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles, store them in a cool dark place for at least six weeks to allow for sedimentation before filtering and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mojave yucca tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that target the affected areas, reducing pain and stiffness. The tincture's unique blend of bioactive compounds works to relax tense muscles and improve joint mobility, allowing for a greater range of motion. Additionally, mojave yucca has natural analgesic properties, which help to alleviate chronic pain associated with frozen shoulder, providing relief and promoting healing without the use of harsh pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mojave Yucca contains compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing pain and swelling associated with Frozen Shoulder.

Analgesic properties: The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, can help relieve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, providing relief from Frozen Shoulder's sharp pains.

Immune system modulators: Mojave Yucca has immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and promoting tissue healing, both of which are crucial for recovering from Frozen Shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility, making them an effective treatment for frozen shoulder.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and lignans, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to relieve pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yucca schidigera roots in the spring or fall, ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested root material in a warm oven set at 150°f for 3-4 hours to preserve its bioactive compounds.
Chop 10 ounces of dried yucca schidigera root into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it two-thirds full.
Store the tincture mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction, before straining and filtering the liquid.
Bottle and store the final tincture at room temperature, using 20-30 drops twice a day as needed, or as directed by a qualified healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce muscle spasms and ease pain in the affected area. Additionally, the relaxing effects of English lavender can promote better sleep, which is often disrupted by the discomfort and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, English lavender tinctures can provide relief from the chronic pain and limited mobility characteristic of this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with frozen shoulder, promoting comfort and relaxation.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of lavender may help alleviate pain and discomfort in the affected joint, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness.

Sedative and calming: Lavender's sedative and calming effects can help reduce stress, anxiety, and muscle tension, which are often exacerbated by frozen shoulder, promoting relaxation and restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and improve circulation to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
Buds: Contains a high concentration of essential oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in amounts of 50 grams for preparation.
Combine the dried flowers with 70% ethanol in a ratio of 1:3 and steep for 6 weeks.
Shake the mixture daily during the steeping period to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid from the solids using cheesecloth or a coffee filter after 6 weeks have passed.
Transfer the clear tincture into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the affected joint. The menthol content in peppermint also has a cooling effect, which can help relax tense muscles and improve range of motion. Additionally, peppermint's natural analgesic properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities without restriction. This natural remedy can provide relief from the stiffness and pain of frozen shoulder, promoting a faster recovery and improved mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, easing stiffness and discomfort.

Pain relief properties: The analgesic and numbing effects of peppermint tincture can provide rapid pain relief by blocking pain receptors and reducing nerve impulses that transmit pain signals to the brain.

Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds, such as menthol, can help relax muscle spasms and cramps associated with frozen shoulder, promoting smooth joint movement and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, peppermint roots help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making them effective for treating frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of menthol and menthone, peppermint rhyzomes provide a cooling effect that helps ease joint pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in abundance for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a dehydrator or air circulation method for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried peppermint leaves with two parts vodka or ethanol as a solvent in a glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks to allow for gradual extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and sediment for a pure extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The herbs in ginseng tincture, such as panax notoginseng and eleuthero, work together to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, allowing for greater range of motion and reduced discomfort. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to have analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the pain associated with frozen shoulder, making it a natural and effective remedy for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Ginseng tincture helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which is often a contributing factor to frozen shoulder, allowing the body to focus on healing rather than perpetuating inflammation.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The saponins present in ginseng tincture have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, promoting improved joint mobility and function.

Immune-Modulating Properties: Ginseng tincture has immunomodulatory effects that can help regulate the immune response, which is often dysregulated in cases of frozen shoulder, leading to chronic inflammation and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginseng plant are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective for alleviating symptoms of frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of ginseng contain saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility, making them useful for treating frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest panax ginseng roots from a trusted source or purchase high-quality dried roots.
Cut the panax ginseng roots into small pieces and soak them in 80-proof vodka for four weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and combine it with distilled water to make a 1:5 ratio tincture mixture.
Transfer the tincture mixture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dry place for six months to allow for maturation.
Once matured, use 20-30 drops of the panax ginseng tincture three times daily to help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that target the underlying inflammatory process. The tincture's potent anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness. Additionally, hops' ability to modulate the body's stress response can help alleviate mental and emotional tension, which is often linked to frozen shoulder. By addressing both physical and emotional aspects of the condition, herbal hops tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Ant-inflammatory: Hops tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as humulone and lupulone, can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, thereby alleviating symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Pain-relieving: The tincture contains a small amount of myrcene, a natural analgesic that can help numb pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to move the affected joint.
Antispasmodic: Hops tincture's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and reduce stiffness in the shoulder region, which is often a hallmark symptom of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: Contains glycosides and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hops flowers from a trusted source and wash them gently under cold running water.
Combine 100g of dried hops flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking occasionally every other day.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Store the humulus lupulus tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 2 years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder are liquid extracts made from plants that help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint. These tinctures work by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and relaxing muscles. The best herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder include Capsicum annuum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Arnica montana, Zingiber officinale, Rhus toxicodendron, Boswellia serrata, Arctium lappa, Vitex agnus-castus, and Cinchona officinalis. If left untreated, frozen shoulder can significantly impact daily life, causing chronic pain, limited mobility, and emotional distress, affecting one's ability to work, perform daily tasks, and enjoy activities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions characterized by inflammation, stiffness, and limited mobility. Examples include tendinitis, bursitis, and carpal tunnel syndrome. Herbal tinctures like Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens) and Turmeric (Curcuma longa) help reduce pain and inflammation, while tinctures made from Willow Bark (Salix alba) and Ginger (Zingiber officinale) provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These natural remedies can be used in combination with physical therapy and other treatments to alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with frozen shoulder is a blend of Turmeric, Ginger, and Arnica. Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound, while Ginger aids in reducing pain and swelling. Arnica, known for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helps to reduce stiffness and promote healing. By combining these three tinctures, you can create a synergistic effect that targets the root causes of frozen shoulder, promoting relaxation, reducing pain, and facilitating recovery.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The capsaicin in Capsicum annuum stimulates blood flow and reduces pain and inflammation in the affected joint. This increased blood flow and reduced inflammation help to relax and mobilize the shoulder muscles, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness. As a result, patients often experience relief from the pain and mobility limitations associated with frozen shoulder. Regular use may aid in the recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with frozen shoulder by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing inflammation and pain.

Capsaicinoids: Capsaicinoids, particularly capsaicin, help with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the activity of inflammatory enzymes and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to joint pain and inflammation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, help with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum are primarily used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of capsaicin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Capsicum annuum leaves are used to make tinctures to take advantage of their anti-inflammatory properties and to provide relief from the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are used in tinctures to harness their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate the pain and discomfort of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried capsicum annuum, 70% ethanol, and a glass jar with a lid for tincture preparation.
Combine the dried capsicum annuum with 500ml of 70% ethanol in the glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another glass container, discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before use, shaking the container daily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling. The licorice root in the tincture is known for its ability to soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints. This natural remedy can also help improve range of motion and flexibility by reducing stiffness and tension in the affected area. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder and promote healing, making it a popular natural alternative for pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected shoulder joint, alleviating pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Flavonoids: Act as powerful antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the shoulder joint, thereby promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Triterpenoids (specifically Glycyrrhetic acid): Have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, allowing for improved mobility and range of motion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help reduce inflammation and pain.
Stems: Stems from Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Leaves are used in tinctures for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra in a clean environment to ensure quality.
Cut the dried root into small pieces weighing 1 part to 2 parts of 95 ethanol.
Steep the root pieces in the ethanol mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It contains curcumin, a compound that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing pain and swelling in the affected area. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to improve joint mobility and reduce stiffness, making it easier to move the shoulder joint. By reducing inflammation and improving joint function, Curcuma longa tinctures can help alleviate symptoms associated with frozen shoulder, promoting faster recovery and improving overall quality of life. Regular use may also enhance long-term joint health.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Turmerones: These sesquiterpenes have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder.
Volatile oils (e.g., sesquiterpene alcohols): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain, inflammation, and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most used part because they contain the highest concentration of curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are another commonly used part due to their rich curcumin content, which helps alleviate pain and reduces inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Leaves are also used in tinctures for their bioactive compounds, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature rhizomes of curcuma longa when they are dry and free of moisture.
Clean the rhizomes thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it gently every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The tincture's ability to promote blood flow and reduce scar tissue also helps to break down adhesions that can cause stiffness and limited mobility. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Arnica montana tinctures can help to alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for greater range of motion and improved overall function. This can lead to faster recovery and improved quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Helps to reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by modulating the immune system and reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Helenalin: Exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which can help to reduce pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Thiophene derivatives (e.g., arnidiol, arnicin): Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by scavenging free radicals and modulating the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for frozen shoulder treatment.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

Roots: The roots are used because they contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds than the flowers, providing enhanced anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and the plant is in a sunny location.
Dry the harvested arnica montana flowers and leaves using paper bags or a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Chop the dried arnica montana flowers and leaves into small pieces using a knife or scissors to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped dried arnica montana with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent and let steep for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass container to separate the tincture from the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce pain. The tincture, often derived from ginger root, contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. By reducing inflammation and pain, Zingiber officinale tinctures can help improve range of motion and promote healing in frozen shoulder. Its warming properties may also aid in relaxing tense muscles, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory properties that aid in the reduction of pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Zingibain: This compound has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to produce tinctures for frozen shoulder as they contain a significant amount of essential oils, including gingerol and shogaol, which help in reducing pain and inflammation.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are used to make tinctures for frozen shoulder as they contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, which aid in reducing pain and inflammation associated with the condition.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhizomes in late summer or early fall when they are mature and the tops have started to yellow.
Wash the rhizomes thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces using a knife or a sharp tool to increase their surface area.
Steep the chopped rhizomes in a glass jar with 80 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhus toxicodendron tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it reduces inflammation and relaxes the muscles surrounding the shoulder joint, thereby improving range of motion. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for greater mobility and flexibility. Additionally, Rhus toxicodendron is known to stimulate the body's natural healing processes, promoting the regeneration of damaged tissues and facilitating the recovery process, ultimately leading to improved shoulder function and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key factors contributing to the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Phenolic compounds: Phenolic compounds in Rhus toxicodendron have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and promote tissue repair in frozen shoulder.

Glycosides: Glycosides in Rhus toxicodendron have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic activities, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder, and promote the healing process.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rhus toxicodendron contain urushiol, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that might help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Rhus toxicodendron are believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that might help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: The seeds of Rhus toxicodendron contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that might help reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh rhus toxicodendron leaves and stems with a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts solvent.
Chop the plant material to release its medicinal properties and expose it to air for 2-3 days.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 cups of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every 2-3 days.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label it for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. It contains compounds like boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected joint. This natural remedy helps to reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Additionally, Boswellia serrata tinctures has been traditionally used to aid in the reduction of scar tissue and adhesions, further contributing to its effectiveness in relieving frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids: Help reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint, allowing for increased mobility and reduced pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Acyl-Phloroglucinols: Inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, leading to a decrease in pain and swelling in the affected shoulder.
Acids (including boswellic acids): Modulate the immune system's response, reducing inflammation and preventing tissue damage in the shoulder joint, which contributes to frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which are believed to reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Stems: The stems are also used, as they contain boswellic acids and other active compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin, 1 liter of 80% ethanol, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried resin with 1 liter of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar, mix well.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discard the solids.
Filter the tincture again through a coffee filter and transfer it to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arctium lappa tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it promotes inflammation reduction and relaxes muscles in the affected area. The anti-inflammatory properties of burdock root, the active ingredient in Arctium lappa, can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, the tincture's analgesic properties can help reduce discomfort and promote relaxation, allowing the shoulder joint to move freely once more. This can lead to improved range of motion and reduced symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Arctiin: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint.
Phenolic acids: Possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Arctium lappa are widely used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: The seeds of Arctium lappa are used to make tinctures as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.

Leaves: The leaves of Arctium lappa are used to make tinctures due to their ant-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce pain, inflammation, and muscle spasms associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh roots from the plant with a fork to prevent damage.
Chop the fresh roots into small pieces and weigh 1 part of them.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling in the affected joint. The tincture is thought to increase blood flow to the area, promoting healing and relaxation of the muscles. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus is believed to balance hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to frozen shoulder. The tincture's natural compounds may also help to reduce stiffness and improve range of motion, making it a promising alternative treatment for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to frozen shoulder.
Agnusides: These iridoid glycosides have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of iridoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their oil content, which contains fatty acids that may help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected shoulder joint.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried vitex agnus-castus berries from a clean source and store them in a paper bag.
Combine the dried vitex agnus-castus berries with 80-100 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the vitex agnus-castus and solvent mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the vitex agnus-castus tincture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks before using it as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms. The alkaloids present in Cinchona officinalis, such as quinine, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that ease pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, the tincture's ability to relax muscle spasms helps restore mobility and range of motion, allowing for a more comfortable and effective recovery. This natural remedy offers a holistic approach to managing frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Quinidine: Quinidine helps by reducing inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the release of pain-causing chemicals and reducing muscle spasms.
Rubine (Anthraquinone): Rubine helps by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged tissues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis contain quinine, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The bark of Cinchona officinalis is rich in quinine and other alkaloids that reduce inflammation and ease pain in frozen shoulder.
Stems: The stems of Cinchona officinalis contain quinine and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark in the early morning after the rain and dry it for at least six hours.
Chop 100 grams of the dried cinchona officinalis bark into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar.
Add 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka to the jar with the chopped cinchona officinalis bark.
Seal the jar and steep the mixture for two weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
